claim: The local proposal reports 20.3% token savings, 36.4% fewer API calls, 49.9% fewer cache reads, 39.4% lower wall time, and no measured recall loss for fish-trail A/B testing.
notes: Use as local headline result, but mark as single-model / small-N.

claim: The main A/B experiment used 21 interleaved messages across python-setup, database, and cicd, with three recall questions and claude-sonnet-4.
notes: Defines current experimental boundary.

claim: The strongest mechanism evidence is the API-call reduction from 140 to 89, because token savings appear to follow from changed trajectory rather than smaller compaction summaries alone.
notes: Supported by analysis section and metrics table; still needs trajectory inspection.

claim: System-prompt injection reduced compaction count from two to one in the tested runs and avoided rule reads becoming uncached conversation context.
notes: Separate but related result: cached instruction placement affects context growth.

claim: LLMLingua represents prompt compression work aimed at reducing prompt size while preserving semantic utility.
notes: Use as passive text compression baseline family.

claim: ACON optimizes context compression for long-horizon agents and reports 26-54% peak-token reduction while largely preserving performance.
notes: Closest external comparator for agent-context optimization.

claim: SWE-Pruner targets coding-agent contexts and reports 23-54% token reduction across agent tasks with minimal performance impact.
notes: Relevant comparator in coding-agent setting.

claim: Membox introduces Topic Loom, grouping consecutive same-topic turns into coherent memory boxes at storage time.
notes: Relevant to topic-contiguous memory, but it works at memory storage rather than compaction-time prompt restructuring.

claim: Context-Agent models multi-turn dialogue history as a dynamic tree to handle non-linear topic shifts and improve context efficiency.
notes: Supports broader claim that linear conversation history is a poor fit for topic-shifting dialogue.

claim: The revised proposal explicitly avoids claiming weak models equal strong models, and instead studies when stable harnesses reduce sensitivity to model capability differences.
notes: Use as corrected thesis boundary.

claim: The proposal defines compression ratio as one minus harness gap divided by baseline gap across models.
notes: Core metric candidate.

claim: PEtFiSh should add TaskSpec, EvidenceBundle, MemorySlice, and OutputContract as model-independent task-control objects.
notes: Strong architecture direction, not yet validated experimentally.

claim: The main engineering risk is contract drift across manifests, registries, installers, docs, AGENTS rules, and tests.
notes: Supported by local survey's petfish.ai review and issue analysis.

claim: Anthropic distinguishes predictable workflows from more autonomous agents, implying that stable task structure should be encoded as workflow where possible.
notes: Use to support harness strength selection.

claim: LangGraph emphasizes durable execution, persistence, memory, and human-in-the-loop workflows for agent orchestration.
notes: Comparator runtime for harness implementation.

claim: DSPy abstracts LM pipelines as declarative modules and computational graphs that can be optimized, moving beyond hand-written prompts.
notes: Supports 'program, don't only prompt' angle.

claim: AgentSPEX proposes explicit control flow, typed steps, state management, verification, logging, and a customizable agent harness.
notes: Directly relevant to contract-driven workflow specification.

claim: Guardrails validators encode criteria for LLM outputs and specify actions when outputs fail validation.
notes: Supports verification layer.

claim: A token-only trajectory screen over the existing A/B JSON labels 17 baseline calls versus 11 plugin calls as large_tool_or_state_rebuild candidates.
notes: Generated by research/04_methods/scripts/label_token_trajectory.py. Heuristic only; requires full message/tool text before being used as semantic evidence.

claim: The benchmark metric helper computes cross-model gap compression ratios from run-level metrics and can distinguish baseline arm gaps from harness arm gaps.
notes: Validated on synthetic sample-harness-runs.json only; not a real benchmark result.

claim: The full benchmark infrastructure can compile 240 planned runs into packet summaries, generate 240 deterministic mock run metrics, and compute per-arm gap-compression summaries without pipeline errors.
notes: Infrastructure validation only. Uses mock model tiers and synthetic scores; must not be cited as empirical model evidence.

claim: The first real-model benchmark slice is prepared as 24 planned runs across two fixtures, two model tiers, two harness arms, and three repetitions, with per-run artifact directories created.
notes: Preparation evidence only. No real model calls have been run yet.

claim: The first-slice evaluator can score golden outputs for structured extraction and project initialization, producing expected high metrics while leaving pending real-run artifacts at zero/pending.
notes: Evaluator validation only. This tests scoring logic, not model behavior.

claim: The first real-model slice now has rendered prompts and adapter request payloads for all 24 planned runs, plus a human-readable execution queue.
notes: Execution preparation only. No external model has been called yet.

claim: The provider configuration validates and the OpenAI-compatible adapter can dry-run all 24 first-slice runs, mapping strong_model to gpt-5.1 and budget_model to gpt-5.1-mini in the example config.
notes: Dry-run only. Model names are example placeholders and must be confirmed against available provider models before execution.

claim: The metrics collector skips pending first-slice runs based on validation status and successfully collects completed golden-check runs.
notes: Collector validation only. The first-slice output currently has 0 completed runs and 24 pending warnings; the golden-check output has 2 completed runs and 0 warnings. No real model outputs have been produced.

claim: A local-only real-run preflight now checks the first 24-run slice for manifest integrity, adapter request consistency, provider mapping, validation status, and API-key readiness before execution.
notes: Preflight currently reports 0 errors and 2 warnings: the config is still an example file and OPENAI_API_KEY is not set. No network or model call was made.

claim: A local-only postprocess pipeline now evaluates first-slice artifacts, collects only completed metrics, and computes gap-compression metrics only when enough completed baseline and harness runs exist.
notes: Current pending first-slice postprocess reports 0 completed runs, 24 warnings, and metrics_computed=false. This validates the stop condition before real model execution; it is not empirical model evidence.

claim: A four-run smoke manifest selects the minimal structured-extraction subset covering strong_model and budget_model under G0 and G9 for repetition 1.
notes: Local rehearsal only. Smoke preflight reports 0 errors and 2 warnings; smoke postprocess reports 0 completed runs, 4 pending warnings, and metrics_computed=false because no model calls have been made.

claim: OpenAI's official model docs list gpt-5.1 and gpt-5-mini as model IDs, and both model pages list Chat Completions as a supported endpoint.
notes: Used only to revise the provider mapping before execution. Actual account access and rate limits still need runtime confirmation.

claim: The reviewed OpenAI config maps strong_model to gpt-5.1 and budget_model to gpt-5-mini; provider config validation passes and the four-run smoke adapter dry-run processes all runs without network calls.
notes: The require-keys preflight still fails because OPENAI_API_KEY is not set. No real model outputs have been produced.

claim: The smoke workflow wrapper runs preflight, adapter dry-run, and postprocess in sequence, and refuses real execution unless both --execute and --confirm-cost are provided.
notes: Validated in dry-run mode with 4 smoke runs. It made no network/API calls, collected 0 completed runs, and preserved the pending-output stop condition.

claim: SiliconFlow's official docs provide an OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint under /v1/chat/completions and list deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2 and Qwen/Qwen3-8B in the model guide.
notes: Used to define SiliconFlow provider mapping for the smoke pilot. Actual key permissions and account quota still require runtime confirmation.

claim: The reviewed SiliconFlow config maps strong_model to deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2 and budget_model to Qwen/Qwen3-8B; config validation, adapter dry-run, and guarded smoke workflow dry-run all pass without network calls.
notes: Preflight reports 0 errors and 1 warning because SILICONFLOW_API_KEY is not set. No real model outputs have been produced.

claim: The first real SiliconFlow smoke pilot executed 4 structured-extraction runs successfully, collected 4 completed metric payloads, and produced 0 collection warnings.
notes: Models: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2 for strong_model and Qwen/Qwen3-8B for budget_model. This is real model evidence, but only for one fixture and one repetition.

claim: In the smoke pilot, G9 improved schema validity and citation grounding relative to G0 for both model tiers, but the gap-compression ratio is not interpretable because the G0 baseline cross-model gap was zero for all tracked metrics.
notes: G0 task_success was 0.333 for both model tiers; G9 task_success was 0.667 for strong_model and 1.000 for budget_model. Baseline gap zero makes compression_ratio n/a, so this supports output-contract sanity but not the main gap-compression hypothesis.

claim: The full SiliconFlow v2 slice executed 24 runs, collected 24 completed metric payloads, produced 0 collection warnings, and computed gap-compression metrics.
notes: One Qwen/Qwen3-8B hard/G0 run timed out on the first attempt and was rerun successfully. The final postprocess uses 24 completed runs.

claim: In the full SiliconFlow v2 slice, G9 reduced cross-model gap to 0 for task_success, schema_validity, tool_call_correctness, human_acceptance, cost_efficiency, and safety_consistency, yielding compression_ratio 1.000 for those metrics with nonzero G0 baseline gap.
notes: Baseline G0 gaps were 0.117 task_success, 0.194 schema_validity, 0.500 tool_call_correctness, 0.156 human_acceptance, 0.117 cost_efficiency, and 0.500 safety_consistency. Citation grounding baseline gap was 0 and remains n/a.

claim: The SiliconFlow project-initialization slice executed 12 runs, collected 12 completed metric payloads, produced 0 collection warnings, and computed gap-compression metrics.
notes: The outer workflow timed out during postprocess waiting, but adapter output shows 12 executed runs and manual final postprocess succeeded with 12 completed runs.

claim: For project initialization, G9 compressed the task_success and safety_consistency cross-model gaps to 0, but widened schema_validity, human_acceptance, and cost_efficiency gaps because Qwen/Qwen3-8B benefited more strongly from the harness than DeepSeek-V3.2.
notes: Group means: budget_model G0/G9 task_success 0.778/1.000 and schema_validity 0.250/1.000; strong_model G0/G9 task_success 0.889/1.000 and schema_validity 0.000/0.417. This is mixed but still supports absolute harness benefit.

claim: The SiliconFlow research-workflow slice executed 12 runs, collected 12 completed metric payloads, produced 0 collection warnings, and computed gap-compression metrics.
notes: Task class: evidence-backed research synthesis. Models: DeepSeek-V3.2 as strong_model and Qwen/Qwen3-8B as budget_model. Arms: G0 and G9. Repetitions: 3.

claim: For research workflow, G9 strongly improved absolute task_success, schema_validity, citation_grounding, human_acceptance, cost_efficiency, and safety_consistency for both model tiers, but gap-compression is only interpretable for schema_validity and related aggregate metrics because several G0 baseline gaps were zero.
notes: Group means: strong_model G0/G9 task_success 0.000/1.000, schema_validity 0.400/1.000, citation_grounding 0.000/1.000; budget_model G0/G9 task_success 0.000/0.917, schema_validity 0.333/1.000, citation_grounding 0.000/1.000. Schema gap compressed from 0.067 to 0; task_success gap is n/a because baseline gap was 0.

claim: The correct claim boundary is that contract-rich harnessing improves absolute contract adherence across tested task classes and sometimes compresses cross-model gaps; gap compression is an empirical outcome, not a guaranteed property.
notes: Synthesizes structured-extraction, project-initialization, and research-workflow slices. Avoids the unsupported claim that harnesses generally make budget models equivalent to strong models.

claim: The next evaluation should use mechanism atoms rather than broad task labels, adding weak-model enablement lift and harnessed-weak-vs-unconstrained-strong advantage alongside gap compression.
notes: Methodology decision derived from prior SiliconFlow slices. This is not new model evidence; it defines the next experimental design and claim ladder.

claim: Mechanism atoms should be defined and accepted before composing broad project-initialization or research-workflow tasks; accepted atoms require deterministic pass criteria, a composition interface, and weak-model improvement room.
notes: Adds mechanism_atom.schema.json and allows task_spec.task_type=mechanism_atom. This is a methodology specification, not empirical model evidence.

claim: Mechanism atoms should be designed by covering the three-dimensional space of harness mechanism, agent operation, and failure mode; the first sufficient library should cover seven canonical mechanisms, core operations, dominant failure modes, and both target composed workflows.
notes: Defines a 10-atom minimum sufficient library and acceptance gates. This is a methodology specification, not empirical model evidence.

claim: The next research phase is governed by an 8-stage roadmap that forbids additional broad workflow model slices until mechanism atoms are specified, locally validated, piloted, and composed only after passing atom-level gates.
notes: Stage 1 is complete; Stage 2 is the next active work. This is an execution constraint and methodology governance artifact, not empirical model evidence.

claim: Stage 2 specified all A1-A10 mechanism atoms with mechanism_atom.json, TaskSpec, MemorySlice, EvidenceBundle, OutputContract, input, golden output, known-bad output, pass criteria, and composition interface; both generic fixture validation and mechanism-atom validation passed for 10/10 atoms.
notes: Local specification and validation only. No model/API calls were made, so this is not empirical model behavior evidence.

claim: Stage 3 selected A1, A3, A4, A8, and A6 as first-batch mechanism atoms and confirmed they can compile into 40 full benchmark packets across strong_model, budget_model, G0, G2, G8, and G9 with 0 packet failures.
notes: Infrastructure validation only. This one-repetition dry matrix is not the Stage 6 model pilot and includes no model/API calls.

claim: Stage 4 added a mechanism-atom evaluator, atom-primary metrics, weak-model enablement lift, and harnessed-weak-vs-strong-G0 metric support; first-batch local golden/bad evaluation passed 10/10 expectation checks after fixing an A3 constraint-consistency construct-validity issue and an A8 evidence-bundle mismatch.
notes: Local evaluator validation only. No model/API calls were made.

claim: Stage 5 passed local gates for mechanism-atom execution: 10/10 atom fixtures passed generic and mechanism-specific validation, first-batch golden/bad checks had 0 expectation failures, 40 prompt artifacts exported with preflight PASS, and pending-output postprocess collected 0 completed runs as expected.
notes: Local gate and dry-run validation only. No model/API calls were made; the 40-run manifest is not empirical model evidence.

claim: Stage 6 completed a real 48-run SiliconFlow mechanism-atom pilot across A1 schema-bound extraction, A3 constraint-safe planning, A8 evidence-type separation, strong_model, budget_model, G0, G2, G8, and G9; final postprocess collected 48/48 completed runs with 0 warnings and computed metrics.
notes: Execution deviations occurred due adapter and provider timeouts, but all pending runs were recovered with narrower rerun manifests and longer provider timeout. This is empirical model evidence for the selected three atoms.

claim: The Stage 6 pilot strongly supports weak-model enablement and harnessed-weak-vs-unconstrained-strong advantage on selected mechanism atoms, while gap compression is positive on general contract metrics but mixed on atom-primary metrics.
notes: Aggregate weak-model task_success lift was +0.521 for G2, +0.549 for G8, and +0.576 for G9; weak-vs-strong-G0 task_success advantage was +0.688, +0.715, and +0.743. G9 task_success and schema gap compression were 1.000, but atom_primary_metric compression was mixed.

claim: Full Stage 7 macro-workflow composition is blocked under the strict roadmap because the Stage 6 pilot tested A1, A3, and A8 only; project-initialization and research-workflow composition require additional atoms such as A2, A4, A5, A6, A7, A9, and A10 to be model-tested first.
notes: This is a stage-gate decision, not a negative model result. It prevents overclaiming and adds Stage 6b for composition-critical atoms.

claim: Stage 6b completed a real 42-run SiliconFlow composition-critical mechanism-atom pilot across seven atoms, two model tiers, and G0/G8/G9; final postprocess collected 42/42 completed runs with 0 warnings and computed metrics.
notes: This is empirical model evidence for composition-critical atoms, but it does not by itself pass the macro-composition gate.

claim: Stage 6b supports weak-model enablement on broad contract metrics but blocks Stage 7 macro-workflow composition because several composition-critical atoms did not pass or exposed evaluator/fixture construct-validity issues.
notes: Observed issues include brittle A9/A10 evaluator matching, under-specified A6 fixture input, and unresolved A5/A7 atom difficulty. Stage 6c remediation is required before composition.

claim: Stage 6c completed evaluator and fixture remediation, passed local golden/bad regression for A5/A6/A7/A9/A10, recomputed the Stage 6b 42-run outputs with 0 warnings, and reran the revised A6 fixture for 6/6 completed SiliconFlow runs with 0 warnings.
notes: A5 accepted complete/completed normalization; A7 accepted textual traces but retained semantic decision scoring; A9 accepted AGENTS.md as a protected blocked target; A10 accepted broader stale-plan exclusion wording; A6 input now includes the original title field and repair_success is semantic rather than trace-string exact.

claim: After Stage 6c remediation, weak-model enablement and atom-primary gap-compression evidence improved, but full Stage 7 macro-workflow composition remains blocked by remaining non-passing atoms, especially A7 budget-model decision quality and A10 bounded-context recall.
notes: Final 42-run view: G8/G9 weak task_success lift +0.524/+0.542, schema_validity lift +0.833/+0.833, atom_primary_metric lift +0.429/+0.571, task_success compression 0.890/0.435, and atom_primary_metric compression 0.812/0.875. A6 now cleanly passes repair_success under G8/G9 for both models, while G0 remains 0.

claim: Stage 6d passed local regression after correcting A10 field-aware context scoring, A7 object-shaped trace handling, and A5 semantic stage-gated task scoring; final recomputation collected 42/42 Stage 6b outputs and 6/6 revised A6 outputs with 0 warnings.
notes: No additional model/API runs were needed in Stage 6d; this was a corrected evaluation and gate-decision stage over existing real model outputs.

claim: Stage 6d allows only a partial Stage 7p composition using A10 bounded context recall, A9 no-overwrite action planning, and A6 validator repair; full project-initialization or research-workflow composition remains blocked by A2/A4/A5/A7 boundary cases.
notes: Final 42-run metrics show G8/G9 weak task_success lift +0.702/+0.661, atom_primary_metric lift +0.714/+0.714, and atom_primary weak-vs-strong-G0 +1.000/+1.000. A6, A9, and A10 pass for both model tiers under G8/G9; A2, A4, A5, and A7 do not.

claim: Stage 7p completed a real 6-run SiliconFlow partial macro-composition slice for A10 -> A9 -> A6 with 6/6 completed runs, 0 collection warnings, and computed metrics after local golden/bad macro evaluator regression passed.
notes: Models were DeepSeek-V3.2 as strong_model and Qwen/Qwen3-8B as budget_model; arms were G0, G8, and G9; repetitions=1.

claim: Stage 7p shows that passing atoms compose successfully for the strong model under G8/G9, while the budget model receives large absolute lift but fails the full chain because explicit stale-context exclusion is not preserved in the composed output.
notes: Strong_model G8/G9 pass the macro chain with task_success=1.000. Budget_model G8/G9 reach task_success 0.800/0.900 and schema_validity=1.000, but chain remains false because context_relevance=0.000. This is evidence that atom success does not automatically guarantee weak-model composition success.

claim: Stage 7p v2 completed a real 6-run SiliconFlow composition-retention ablation for A10 -> A9 -> A6 with 6/6 completed runs, 0 collection warnings, computed metrics, and passing local golden/bad regression.
notes: Stage 7p v2 added explicit bounded_context.excluded_context and carried_obligations requirements while preserving the same narrow macro chain and model/arm matrix as Stage 7p v1.

claim: Explicit cross-step obligation retention repairs the weak/low-cost model's Stage 7p v1 composition failure on the A10 -> A9 -> A6 partial macro task.
notes: In Stage 7p v2, both strong_model and budget_model pass under G8/G9 with task_success=1.000, context_relevance=1.000, safety/constraint=1.000, repair_success=1.000, and atom_primary_metric=1.000. G0 fails for both model tiers. This supports a new composition-layer mechanism claim but does not validate full project initialization or full research workflow.

claim: Stage 7r created six revised mechanism atoms, passed 6/6 fixture structure validation, passed 12/12 local golden/bad expectations, compiled 36/36 benchmark packets, passed preflight with 0 errors and 0 warnings, and collected 35/36 real SiliconFlow smoke outputs.
notes: The pending run is a8r-evidence-type-rules__budget_model__G8__r1. A single-run retry and a final controlled retry with timeout_seconds=900 and max_output_tokens=600 both failed with SiliconFlow read timeout on Qwen/Qwen3-8B, so the run is treated as an execution deviation rather than a model-quality score.

claim: Stage 7r supports the mechanism-first redesign direction: under G8/G9, the strong model passes all revised atoms, while the low-cost model gains large absolute task-success improvement but still has specific boundary failures in unsupported-claim grounding and evidence-complete decision traces.
notes: Completed-run metrics: strong_model G8/G9 pass 6/6 with avg task_success=1.000 and avg atom_primary=1.000; budget_model G0 passes 0/6 with avg task_success=0.228; budget_model G8 completes 5/6 and passes 3/5 with avg task_success=0.800; budget_model G9 completes 6/6 and passes 4/6 with avg task_success=0.833. A2R and A7R remain low-cost-model optimization targets before broad workflow composition.

claim: Stage 7r.1 added incremental per-run adapter event logging, created tightened A2R1/A7R1 fixtures, passed 2/2 structure validation, passed 4/4 local golden/bad expectations, compiled an 8-run low-cost-model G8/G9 targeted smoke, exported prompts, and passed preflight with 0 errors and 0 warnings.
notes: No real model API execution was performed in this step. The prepared targeted smoke covers A2R1 and A7R1 with budget_model, G8/G9, and two repetitions.

claim: Stage 7r.1 operationalizes the Stage 7r failure analysis by converting low-cost-model A2R/A7R failures into stricter output contracts and known-bad regression cases before any broader macro composition.
notes: A2R1 directly targets global-only evidence lists and bare grounded-claim strings. A7R1 directly targets missing evidence-linked rejection trace coverage for C3. These are mechanism-level repairs rather than macro-workflow changes.

claim: Stage 7r.1 completed an 8-run SiliconFlow targeted smoke for A2R1 and A7R1 using Qwen/Qwen3-8B under G8/G9 with two repetitions each; final evaluation collected 8/8 completed runs and 8/8 passed runs with task_success=1.000 and atom_primary_metric=1.000 for every run.
notes: The initial 8-run execution completed 7/8 and left A7R1 budget_model G9 r2 pending after provider_request_start. A single-run retry with timeout_seconds=900 and max_output_tokens=600 completed successfully in 103015 ms. Incremental adapter event logs captured the execution path.

claim: Stage 7r.1 supports the mechanism-first repair hypothesis: low-cost-model failures on A2R claim grounding and A7R decision trace completeness can be repaired by tightening output contracts into explicit claim-level evidence binding and rejected-option trace obligations.
notes: This evidence applies to targeted mechanism atoms only. It does not by itself validate broad project-initialization or research-workflow macro tasks, but it removes two Stage 7r low-cost-model blockers for future narrow composition.

claim: Stage 7r.2 admits a narrow evidence-bound decision macro composed from A4R, A2R1, A8R, A7R1, and A5R, while continuing to block full project initialization and full research workflow composition.
notes: The gate uses Stage 7p v2, Stage 7r, and Stage 7r.1 evidence. A2R1 and A7R1 replace the original A2R/A7 for low-cost-model composition. A8R is conditionally admitted because low-cost G8 has a provider-timeout deviation, while low-cost G9 and strong G8/G9 passed.

claim: Stage 7e completed a real 6-run SiliconFlow smoke for the narrow evidence-bound decision macro with 6/6 executed runs and no provider timeout; strong_model G8/G9 and low-cost-model G8 passed with task_success=1.000 and atom_primary_metric=1.000, while both G0 baselines failed and low-cost-model G9 partially failed with task_success=0.714.
notes: The evaluator was corrected after initial scoring to accept construct-valid JSON variants such as uppercase typed_evidence buckets, Stage 7e spacing variants, and short evidence references. Local golden/bad regression still passed after the correction, and the known-bad stale/full-claim output remained rejected.

claim: Stage 7e supports mechanism-bound weak-model enablement for a fixed evidence-decision macro under G8, but it does not support universal model-gap closure, production readiness, or full open-ended project/research workflow composition.
notes: The low-cost-model G8 result matched strong-model G8/G9 on the narrow macro, while low-cost-model G9 missed complete stage-gate and decision-trace retention. The next step should target this retention miss before broader macro expansion.

claim: Stage 7e v2 completed a real 4-run SiliconFlow targeted smoke for the low-cost model under G8/G9 with two repetitions each; 4/4 runs executed with no provider timeout, and all 4/4 runs achieved trace_completeness=1.000 and stage_completion=1.000.
notes: The targeted baseline was Stage 7e v1 low-cost-model G9 with trace_completeness=0.000 and stage_completion=0.000. Stage 7e v2 added explicit retention requirements for decision_trace, stage_gate, and carried_obligations.

claim: Stage 7e v2 supports the targeted retention-repair claim but does not yet establish full macro stability because 3/4 runs still failed strict atom_primary_metric due state_inventory omissions for Git branch, CI status, and network/API approval unknowns.
notes: Full macro pass was 1/4. Non-passing runs had task_success=0.857 and passed schema, grounding, evidence typing, trace, gate, and context relevance, but failed state_accuracy. The next patch should target A4R-style unknown-state retention.

claim: Stage 7e v3 completed a real 4-run SiliconFlow targeted smoke for the low-cost model under G8/G9 with two repetitions each; 4/4 runs executed with no provider timeout, 4/4 preserved the required Git/CI/network unknown-state and forbidden-inference fields, and 3/4 passed the full strict macro evaluation.
notes: Final evaluation after contract-based evidence-combo correction: G8 r1 task_success=0.857 and atom_primary=0.000 due broader known-state provenance omission; G8 r2, G9 r1, and G9 r2 all achieved task_success=1.000 and atom_primary=1.000.

claim: Stage 7e v3 supports the targeted unknown-state retention repair claim, but full macro expansion remains blocked because one G8 run compressed known-state provenance and failed strict state_accuracy.
notes: The remaining failure is narrower than Stage 7e v2: unknown-state retention, trace retention, and stage-gate retention were repaired, but known-state provenance still needs a contract requiring explicit Stage 7r.1/7r.2/7e-v2 evidence-linked known-state items.

claim: Stage 7e v4 completed a real 4-run SiliconFlow targeted smoke for the low-cost model under G8/G9 with two repetitions each; after retrying one provider timeout, all 4/4 runs completed and passed with task_success=1.000 and atom_primary_metric=1.000.
notes: Initial G9 r2 failed with a 420-second read timeout. A first retry with max_output_tokens=600 returned truncated JSON. A second retry with timeout_seconds=900 and max_output_tokens=2000 completed successfully. Local v1-v4 golden/bad regression passed 8/8 expectations after evaluator construct-validity corrections.

claim: Stage 7e v4 supports the narrow mechanism-composition claim that low-cost-model G8/G9 can complete a fixed evidence-bound decision macro when known-state provenance, unknown-state retention, evidence binding, decision trace, stage gate, and carried obligations are explicit.
notes: This claim is limited to fixed-input, no-tool, deterministic evidence-decision macros. It does not validate full research workflow, full project initialization, production readiness, or universal model-gap closure.

claim: The claim-boundary memo was updated to include Stage 7e v1-v4 as a mechanism-bound repair-loop case while preserving explicit non-claims for universal gap closure, full workflow readiness, and production readiness.
notes: The revised memo separates task-slice claims from mechanism-composition claims and adds drafting rules for weak-model enablement claims.

claim: The next research phase should treat Stage 7e v4 as the current admitted macro baseline, draft the methods section, and admit only bounded next macros that reuse passed obligations and declare non-claims before real-model execution.
notes: This is a methodology governance recommendation, not empirical model evidence. Candidate next macros are evidence-bound method-plan update and citation-bound synthesis update.

claim: Stage 7-next built a bounded evidence-bound method-plan update macro and passed local golden/bad validation with 2/2 expectation outcomes met: the golden output passed with task_success=1.000 and atom_primary_metric=1.000, while the premature broader-workflow expansion known-bad output failed with task_success=0.000.
notes: The evaluator was generalized to read fixture-specific evidence type and stage-gate rules from output_contract.json; Stage 7e v4 regression still passed after this evaluator change.

claim: A targeted low-cost-model Stage 7-next smoke is admissible after local gate success, but full project initialization and full research workflow remain blocked until real-model evidence supports broader composition.
notes: Recommended slice: low-cost model only, G8/G9, two repetitions each, event logging enabled, with stop-and-review on systematic contract misses or repeated provider timeout.

claim: Stage 7-next completed a real 4-run SiliconFlow smoke for the low-cost model under G8/G9 with two repetitions each; all 4/4 runs executed without provider errors and passed with task_success=1.000 and atom_primary_metric=1.000.
notes: Run elapsed times were 95.063s, 104.687s, 120.688s, and 212.469s. No timeout or truncated-output retry was needed.

claim: Stage 7-next supports the narrow transfer claim that Stage 7e v4 obligations can be reused to make a low-cost model complete a closely related fixed evidence-bound method-plan update macro with one new explicit method-plan stressor.
notes: This strengthens the mechanism-first repair-loop story but remains limited to fixed-input, no-tool, deterministic macros. It does not support full workflow readiness, production readiness, or universal model-gap closure.

claim: Stage 8 produced a methodology paper methods draft and a claim-evaluation map that frame the study around contract objects, mechanism atoms, admitted macros, local golden/bad gates, targeted real-model smoke tests, and evidence-bound claim decisions.
notes: The draft explicitly separates task-slice claims, mechanism-atom claims, admitted macro claims, and non-claims such as production readiness and full workflow readiness.

claim: The paper should position weak-model enablement on bounded contract-critical operations as the central stable claim, with gap compression treated as conditional evidence rather than the universal thesis.
notes: This proposed framing is grounded in the Stage 7e v1-v4 and Stage 7-next repair-loop evidence, while preserving mixed or n/a gap-compression results from broader slices.

claim: The contract-driven harness paper front matter draft was created with title candidates, an abstract seed, introduction, contributions, contribution-to-evaluation alignment, and explicit non-claims.
notes: This is a drafting artifact, not new empirical evidence. It uses weak-model enablement on bounded contract-critical operations as the central stable framing and treats gap compression as conditional.

claim: The contract-driven harness paper results section draft was created, organizing results from task slices, mechanism atoms, partial macro composition, Stage 7e repair loop, Stage 7-next transfer, and claim boundaries.
notes: This is a drafting artifact, not new empirical evidence. It explicitly includes negative and mixed results, including project-initialization/research-workflow mixed gap movement and Stage 7p v1 composition failure.

claim: The contract-driven harness paper discussion and limitations draft was created, explaining conditional gap compression, why negative results matter, bounded macro limits, deterministic evaluation limits, small-sample smoke-test limits, provider/runtime effects, PEtFiSh specificity, harness cost, and future-work directions.
notes: This is a drafting artifact, not new empirical evidence. It preserves the boundary that current results support bounded contract-critical weak-model enablement, not full workflow or production readiness.

claim: The contract-driven harness paper related work draft was created, positioning the method against agent orchestration, declarative LM programs, structured outputs and validators, retrieval and tool augmentation, memory governance, agent evaluation and verification, and skill or capability ecosystems.
notes: This is a drafting artifact, not new empirical evidence. It frames the paper as a mechanism-first evaluation and repair-loop contribution rather than as a claim of universal model-gap closure or production readiness.

claim: The contract-driven harness methodology paper was assembled into a working full draft with abstract, introduction, related work, methods, results, discussion, conclusion, non-claims, and contribution-evaluation alignment.
notes: This is a drafting artifact, not new empirical evidence. It integrates previously drafted sections and preserves the bounded claim: weak-model enablement on fixed, contract-critical operations, with gap compression treated as conditional.

claim: OpenAI Structured Outputs documentation supports using JSON Schema-based constraints to make model outputs follow a supplied structure, while the paper should avoid treating schema adherence as semantic truth.
notes: Used for related-work positioning of structured-output mechanisms; semantic-grounding limits are handled by the paper's evaluator and limitation language.

claim: Outlines provides structured generation and constrained decoding mechanisms such as schema- or grammar-oriented generation, supporting the related-work category of format-level externalization.
notes: Used only as related-work context for constrained generation, not as empirical evidence for the harness experiments.

claim: AutoGen documentation positions AutoGen as a framework for multi-agent applications with AgentChat, Core, Studio, extensions, and tool/MCP integrations.
notes: Used for related-work positioning of orchestration and multi-agent runtime frameworks.

claim: Semantic Kernel documentation positions the system as lightweight middleware for AI agents, exposing kernels, plugins, observability, and function-calling workflows.
notes: Used for related-work positioning of orchestration/runtime middleware.

claim: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines parametric generation with non-parametric retrieved memory for knowledge-intensive tasks, supporting the paper's externalized-knowledge related-work framing.
notes: Used as background for retrieval as an externalized capability, not as evidence for current fixed-input macro results.

claim: ReAct interleaves reasoning and acting so language models can interact with external information sources, supporting the related-work framing of externalized action and observation.
notes: Used as background for tool/action augmentation; current admitted macros remain no-tool tasks.

claim: Toolformer studies language models learning to use external APIs, supporting the related-work claim that tool access can externalize some model capabilities.
notes: Used as background for tool-use lineage, not as direct evidence for the paper's fixed macro experiments.

claim: Gorilla studies API call generation for large language models and APIBench-style evaluation, supporting the paper's tool/interface externalization related-work category.
notes: Used as background for API/tool-call systems; not used to claim current live-tool workflow readiness.

claim: MemGPT proposes virtual context management and hierarchical memory for LLM agents, supporting the related-work category of memory and context governance.
notes: Used as background for memory systems; the paper's experiments use bounded memory slices rather than full persistent memory.

claim: Letta documentation describes stateful agents with persistent memory, messages, tools, runs, and steps, supporting the related-work framing of memory as governed state.
notes: Used as background for memory system design, not as evidence for the current PEtFiSh-specific harness results.

claim: OAgents-style evaluation work highlights that agent design choices and protocols can produce high variance, supporting the paper's emphasis on deterministic local gates, event logs, and bounded claims.
notes: Used as related-work support for evaluation caution and reproducibility limits.

claim: Semantic Integrity Constraints frame declarative constraints as guardrails for AI-augmented systems, supporting the paper's verification-and-constraint related-work category.
notes: Used as related-work context for semantic constraints; not direct evidence for PEtFiSh macro results.

claim: Agentproof studies static verification of agent workflow graphs and policies, supporting the related-work category of workflow verification and safety checking.
notes: Used as related-work context for verification; current work uses deterministic artifact evaluators rather than formal static verification.

claim: LlamaFirewall proposes a security-focused guardrail framework for AI agents, supporting the related-work category of runtime safety and guardrail systems.
notes: Used as related-work context for agent safety; current experiments do not establish production security readiness.

claim: The full contract-driven harness paper draft passed an internal citation/evidence audit conditionally: all central empirical and related-work claims now have claim-to-evidence-to-source chains, but formal inline citations, exact locators, access dates, and bibliography entries are still required before external submission.
notes: The audit produced citation-audit, unsupported-claims, and source-coverage review files. No blocking unsupported critical claim was found after adding missing related-work source IDs and Appendix C evidence traceability to the paper draft.

claim: AgentSpec is registered as related work on portable declarative agent specification, supporting the paper's comparison between mechanism atoms and declarative agent specifications.
notes: Used as related-work context only; current experiments do not evaluate AgentSpec directly.

claim: The full contract-driven harness paper draft was updated with internal inline evidence/source citations and a References section; all 60 evidence references and 40 source references in the draft resolve to existing ledger and source-index entries.
notes: This completes the internal citation insertion step. The draft still needs conversion to a target venue citation style, exact locators, and access-date/version metadata before external submission.

claim: A venue-neutral citation metadata file was created for the contract-driven harness paper, adding access dates for mutable external documentation, stable external URLs, local experiment artifact locators, and a remaining-work checklist for target venue citation conversion.
notes: This completes the access-date and locator metadata step. Target venue citation style remains pending because no conference, journal, or citation style has been selected.

claim: A venue-neutral BibTeX draft and citation-conversion plan were created for the contract-driven harness paper, preserving source-index keys and documenting the remaining steps required for ACM/IEEE/APA/arXiv-style conversion.
notes: This is a citation-preparation artifact, not a final venue-formatted bibliography. Several newer arXiv references still require exact author-list/version normalization before external submission.

claim: The venue-neutral BibTeX draft was normalized to remove placeholder author groups for OAgents, Semantic Integrity Constraints, Agentproof, and LlamaFirewall; a local structural check found 22 entries with required author/title and URL or howpublished fields and no remaining placeholder-author warnings.
notes: The entries are suitable for internal arXiv-style preparation, but final venue conversion should still verify exact author spelling/order and bibliography style requirements.

claim: A compact results appendix was created for the contract-driven harness paper, summarizing broad task slices, Stage 6 mechanism atoms, Stage 7p/v2 composition, Stage 7r/7r.1 atom repairs, Stage 7e v1-v4 repair loop, Stage 7-next transfer macro, and current claim boundaries.
notes: This is a paper-support artifact for venue-neutral review and later ACM/IEEE/arXiv formatting. It does not add new empirical evidence beyond the cited stage artifacts.

claim: An arXiv-style working draft was created from the contract-driven harness paper: body-level internal evidence/source tags were removed or converted, external citations now use BibTeX keys, evidence traceability remains in Appendix C, and a reproducibility package section was added.
notes: A local conversion check found 22 distinct citation keys, 22 BibTeX entries, zero missing BibTeX keys, zero unused BibTeX keys, and zero remaining inline [E:] or [S:] tags. The draft still needs Markdown-to-LaTeX/source-format conversion before public arXiv posting.

claim: An arXiv-compatible LaTeX source package was generated from the arXiv-style Markdown draft, including the main .tex file, BibTeX file, README, and conversion script; a static check found 22 citation keys, 22 BibTeX entries, zero missing keys, zero unused keys, no remaining inline [E:] or [S:] tags, no Markdown pipe-table rows, a proper abstract environment, and normalized section/subsection headings.
notes: This evidence records the static source-package check. PDF compilation was completed afterward and is recorded separately as P2-E106. Final release still requires human PDF layout review.

claim: A user-level MiKTeX 25.12 LaTeX environment was configured, PATH was updated, and the arXiv source package compiled successfully with pdflatex, BibTeX, and two final pdflatex passes into a 17-page PDF; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The generated PDF is `research/06_outputs/arxiv-source/contract-driven-harness-arxiv.pdf` with size 199501 bytes and Letter page size 612 x 792 pt. The compile log still contains 8 overfull hbox warnings, 130 underfull hbox warnings, and 4 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings from dense appendix tables, so human PDF layout review remains pending before public posting.

claim: An external review rated the paper 8.0/10 and concluded that its strongest framing is bounded agent harness reliability: contract-driven harnesses make some low-cost-model failures observable, repairable, and regression-testable, while open-ended workflow reliability, production readiness, and universal gap compression remain unsupported.
notes: The review explicitly states that it did not locally rerun the repository scripts or reproduce experiments. It used the paper PDF, public repository artifacts, Companion Gateway follow-up context, and prior discussion.

claim: The next paper revision should prioritize claim sharpening and artifact readiness before new broad experiments: revise the abstract, introduce model/harness/workflow distinctions, add related-work and result-boundary tables, formalize atom admission criteria, clarify PEtFiSh as implementation context, and update public artifact wording.
notes: This is a revision plan derived from the 2026-06-10 external review. Proposed next experiments are G9 object ablations, Stage 7e/Stage 7-next perturbation suites, a controlled state-mutating macro, and later cross-provider replication.

claim: The v2 arXiv source package was regenerated from the revised Markdown draft and compiled successfully with MiKTeX 25.12 into a 19-page PDF of 206304 bytes; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The compile log still contains 8 overfull hbox warnings, 161 underfull hbox warnings, and 5 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings from long lines and dense tables. Human PDF layout review remains pending before public posting.

claim: A local public-artifact safety scan after the repository was made public found zero files larger than 50MB and zero matches for configured secret/token patterns after excluding known prompt and benchmark artifacts.
notes: This scan reduces obvious public-repository risk but does not prove the absence of all sensitive data. Continued public release should keep secret scans and human review in the artifact gate.

claim: The v3.1 paper revision compressed the main result table, added a metric reading-guide table, added a closest-prior-work paragraph, reduced repetitive targeted-smoke wording, and created a deferred-work register for citation normalization, formal figure conversion, Appendix C placement, and future experiment expansion.
notes: This is a paper-revision artifact, not new empirical evidence. It responds to the v3 review without adding new model runs or changing the core claim boundary.

claim: The v3.1 arXiv source package compiled successfully with MiKTeX 25.12 into a 20-page PDF of 270104 bytes; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The compile log still contains 9 overfull hbox warnings, 169 underfull hbox warnings, and 3 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings from long metric names, result strings, paths, and dense tables. Human PDF layout review and Appendix C/table placement remain pending.

claim: A v3.1 layout patch applied fixed-width ragged-right table columns for the main findings and metric-guide tables, added a metric-scope note and reproducibility rerun-entry wording, and recompiled the paper into a 20-page PDF of 269508 bytes with zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The layout patch reduced underfull hbox warnings from 169 to 5. Overfull hbox warnings remained at 9, but Table 4 now has only one minor 0.8pt warning; remaining larger warnings come from long metric/result strings and bibliography path references.

claim: Before any release-ready arXiv or venue package, the paper should run a separate citation pass that normalizes paper/preprint references, web documentation references, the public reproducibility-package citation, version notes, and BibTeX key stability.
notes: The citation pass is intentionally separate from the v3.1 layout patch and has explicit acceptance criteria in `research/05_analysis/contract-driven-harness-citation-pass-plan.md`.

claim: A strict human-style pass revised the v3.1 arXiv working draft to reduce generic AI-like paper voice, shorten abstract/introduction sentences, reduce repetitive defensive boundary wording, and replace generic method phrases with more direct engineering language while preserving the empirical claim boundary.
notes: This is a presentation-quality revision, not new empirical evidence. The local style checker remained at 59/100 because it flags `harness` as an AI-high-frequency term and reports some acceptable Markdown/title and technical-enumeration sentences as long-sentence findings.

claim: After the strict human-style pass, the v3.1 arXiv source package compiled successfully with MiKTeX 25.12 into a 19-page PDF of 267637 bytes; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The compile log contains 9 overfull hbox warnings, 5 underfull hbox warnings, and 4 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings, mainly from long metric names, result strings, path references, URLs, and dense longtable page splitting. These are layout warnings, not compile blockers.

claim: An external cross-review judged the human-style revision successful and recommended a local precision-calibration pass rather than another full rewrite; the accepted changes target residual lecture-note phrasing, claim-scope precision, and the nonzero-baseline condition for gap compression.
notes: The intake records which suggestions were adopted, reworded, or rejected, including wording that ties the Abstract scope to failures observed in low-cost-model runs. This is editorial evidence, not empirical evidence about model or harness performance.

claim: The v3.1.1 precision-calibration patch compiled successfully with MiKTeX 25.12 into a 20-page PDF of 268210 bytes; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The patch applies selected external style-cross-review recommendations without changing model runs, metrics, empirical claims, or citation keys. The log contains 9 overfull hbox warnings, 5 underfull hbox warnings, and 3 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings.

claim: The v3.1.1 post-humanization paper body was formally frozen after two final wording changes: smoothing the Abstract opening and replacing `gives` with `provides` in Section 4.2.
notes: Citation normalization, table and Appendix layout, venue-template adaptation, and mechanical typesetting remain open. Later body changes require an explicit unfreeze decision and new review entry.

claim: The frozen-body v3.1.1 paper compiled successfully with MiKTeX 25.12 into a 20-page PDF of 268216 bytes; log review found zero LaTeX errors, zero undefined citations, zero undefined references, zero BibTeX warnings, and zero final rerun warnings.
notes: The final wording patch changed only the Abstract opening and one verb in Section 4.2. The frozen reader artifact is `research/06_outputs/contract-driven-harness-paper-v3.1.1-frozen.pdf`. The compile log contains 9 overfull hbox warnings, 5 underfull hbox warnings, and 3 ignored infinite-glue shrinkage warnings.

claim: A security and systems peer review identified three substantive evidence gaps: four-run macro stability, deterministic-gate perturbation robustness, and measured harness overhead; the review's Strong Accept recommendation was treated as editorial judgment rather than evidence of venue readiness.
notes: The response accepts all three criticisms but rejects 30-50 identical repeats as sufficient evidence of task generalization and rejects adding autonomous network-defense claims.

claim: The post-freeze evidence extension should proceed through usage instrumentation, a 30-run two-macro perturbation pilot, a gated 50-run stability extension, and a 40-run model-by-harness overhead matrix, with v3.1.1 remaining frozen and successful new evidence entering a separate v4 draft.
notes: The minimum paid plan is 120 calls if no fixture/evaluator reset is required. Stage B is the first paid gate; Stage C and Stage D require a separate go decision.

claim: Existing Stage 7 artifacts expose prompt/output bytes, latency, timeout, and retry behavior but do not contain provider-reported token usage or exact request cost; Stage 7-next successful latency ranged from 95.047 to 212.469 seconds, while Stage 7e v4 included a 420.094-second timeout and two retries for the final G9 r2 artifact.
notes: The current `cost_efficiency` metric is a task heuristic and must not be interpreted as API billing efficiency. Exact token and cost comparison requires adapter instrumentation and fresh matched runs.

claim: The OpenAI-compatible adapter now preserves provider-reported token usage, raw usage metadata, response/request identifiers, response model metadata, and declared retry lineage; three local unit tests, Python compilation, and a four-entry SiliconFlow dry-run passed without a provider call.
notes: Missing provider usage remains null and is not estimated from byte counts. Perturbation fixtures, evaluator gates, and the dated pricing snapshot remain open before the 30-call Stage B pilot.

claim: Post-freeze Stage A produced ten fixtures across two macros and five representation conditions; 10/10 golden and 18/18 known-bad cases met expectations, four original macro regression cases preserved prior behavior, and the 30-run Qwen3-8B + G9 queue passed preflight and adapter dry-run without a provider call.
notes: The first local gate exposed an evaluator defect: undeclared evidence-field aliases could satisfy known-state provenance through flattened text. The evaluator was repaired to require structured known-state objects and canonical evidence fields. The final 28-case suite also rejects canonical-surface fallback and mixed distractor support. The dated pricing snapshot is complete; Stage B remains blocked pending explicit approval for 30 provider calls.

claim: On 2026-06-13, SiliconFlow's public pricing data listed Qwen/Qwen3-8B prompt and completion token prices at ¥0 per million tokens and listed deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2 at ¥2 input, ¥3 output, and ¥0.20 cache per million tokens; Qwen/Qwen3-8B was present in the authenticated text-model list.
notes: This is a dated public-list-price and availability observation, not a guarantee of future or account-specific billing. No credential, balance, account identifier, or raw authenticated response was retained.

claim: Stage B should use only Qwen/Qwen3-8B under G9 with temperature 0, max_tokens 2000, and enable_thinking=false; reasoning_effort and thinking_budget should not be sent, and any thinking-mode comparison should be a separate ablation.
notes: This freezes inference mode before paid execution so schema perturbation remains the only experimental factor. SiliconFlow documents reasoning_effort as specific to DeepSeek-V4-Flash rather than Qwen3-8B.

claim: Stage B completed 30/30 Qwen/Qwen3-8B + G9 SiliconFlow calls with zero provider errors, median latency 60.344 seconds, P90 latency 74.309 seconds, 177546 total tokens, zero reasoning tokens, and two completions reaching the 2000-token limit.
notes: The two limit-reaching outputs were truncated invalid JSON in the Stage 7e v4 field-alias condition. The dated public Qwen3-8B token price was ¥0, but runtime and engineering costs remain nonzero.

claim: After correcting an undeclared carried-obligation status-vocabulary defect, Stage B passed 16/30 runs and 5/10 macro-condition cells; neither macro passed all five conditions, so the preregistered Stage B gate failed.
notes: The raw evaluator scored 0/30 because it rejected the semantically valid status `satisfied` used in 24 outputs. The correction preserved 28/28 perturbation local expectations and 4/4 base macro regression expectations. Corrected failures comprised six unknown-state surface failures, six evidence-combination/trace failures, and two output-budget truncations.

claim: The current G9 macros are locally robust to distractor evidence and mostly robust to evidence-order shuffling, but they are not robust to exact declared surface aliases or stable evidence-combination retention; Stage C and Stage D should remain blocked until a fresh Stage B v2 protocol passes.
notes: Because the evaluator required a post-execution construct-validity correction and five cells failed the 2/3 threshold, the 30 Stage B runs must not be pooled into a later stability estimate.

claim: The Stage B summary passed a manual nine-dimension research quality review with no blocking issue; numerical consistency, negative-result coverage, post-hoc correction disclosure, actionability, and expression quality were checked.
notes: The bundled quality-gate script was incompatible with project-scoped P2 evidence IDs because it accepts only EV-XXXXXX, so its format-level F result was not treated as a substantive report rating.

claim: Stage B v3 completed 30/30 Qwen/Qwen3-8B + G9 SiliconFlow calls with zero provider errors, zero retries, zero JSON parse failures, 261943 total tokens, zero reasoning tokens, 19.906-second median latency, and 25.638-second P90 latency.
notes: All outputs were below the 3000-token limit; completion-token usage ranged from 1603 to 2013. The 2026-06-13 public list-price snapshot recorded Qwen3-8B input and output prices at CNY 0 per million tokens.

claim: Stage B v3 passed 18/30 runs and 6/10 macro-condition cells, so its preregistered requirement that every cell pass at least 2/3 runs was falsified.
notes: Failed cells were Stage 7-next canonical 0/3, Stage 7-next unknown-state paraphrase 0/3, Stage 7e v4 canonical 1/3, and Stage 7e v4 unknown-state paraphrase 0/3. Historical Stage B protocols are not pooled with this result.

claim: The Stage B v3 literal skeleton stabilized JSON hierarchy but did not make exact content slots immutable: nine runs omitted required grounded evidence support and six paraphrase runs rewrote required closed-vocabulary labels.
notes: All twelve failures were complete semantic outputs, so no retry was permitted. The next repair should isolate evidence-array immutability and exact paraphrased-value retention as separate mechanism atoms before another full slice.

claim: An independent result review rated the Stage B v3 record A and found the execution counts, cell decisions, failure taxonomy, Wilson interval, evaluator decision, and claim boundaries consistent with the retained artifacts.
notes: The review approved publication as a negative-result research record, not as evidence of general perturbation robustness. The frozen v3.1.1 paper remains unchanged.

claim: Stage B v4-local created four mechanism fixtures for exact evidence-array immutability and exact closed-vocabulary retention; all four golden outputs passed, all 28 known-bad outputs were rejected, six evaluator unit tests passed, and model-surface isolation reported zero forbidden-value leaks.
notes: Twenty-four known-bad outputs remained schema-valid while receiving atom_primary_metric=0.000, separating content-preservation failures from structural failures. No provider call was made.

claim: The unchanged Stage B v3 local perturbation suite still passed 110/110 golden and known-bad expectations after the Stage B v4 scripts and fixtures were added.
notes: The v4 evaluator is separate from the v3 macro evaluator, so this regression checks that the new local atom work did not alter historical v3 scoring behavior.

claim: The two Stage B v3 exact-content failure mechanisms are now isolated into deterministic, regression-testable atoms, making a small targeted low-cost-model smoke admissible but not yet supporting any model-reliability or perturbation-robustness claim.
notes: Recommended smoke is four fixtures under Qwen/Qwen3-8B + G9 with two repetitions each, for eight provider calls. The smoke is a mechanism debugging gate and must not be pooled with prior Stage B protocols.

claim: Stage B v4-smoke completed 8/8 Qwen/Qwen3-8B + G9 SiliconFlow calls with zero provider errors, zero retries, zero reasoning tokens, 9458 total tokens, and 3.407-8.454 second per-run latency.
notes: The run used temperature 0, enable_thinking=false, and max_output_tokens=2000. Completion usage ranged from 84 to 191 tokens, so no output approached the limit.

claim: All 8/8 Stage B v4-smoke outputs passed schema and exact-retention evaluation; each of the four fixtures passed both repetitions, including the declared evidence-field alias and the paraphrased do_not_guess closed vocabulary that failed systematically in Stage B v3.
notes: This is a mechanism-level result on small fixed-output atoms. It does not establish transfer to the Stage B v3 macros, general perturbation robustness, workflow readiness, or production reliability.

claim: The Stage B v3 exact-content failures are not irreducible Qwen3-8B capability failures: under isolated G9 contracts, the model can preserve the required arrays and labels exactly; the next unresolved question is whether that preservation survives bounded macro recomposition and increased attention load.
notes: The next experiment should compose B4A and B4B into one bounded macro with all other obligations fixed. More repetitions of the isolated atoms would measure repeatability but would not answer the transfer question.

claim: Stage B v4-recomposition-local created canonical and dual-surface-stress bounded macros that compose B4B state-vocabulary retention, B4A evidence-array retention, and one fixed local-first composition gate; both golden outputs passed and all 20 known-bad outputs were rejected with their preregistered component-metric vectors.
notes: The complete local record was 22/22 expectation matches, five evaluator unit tests passed, and model-surface isolation found zero alternative-field or alternative-vocabulary leaks. No provider call was made.

claim: The bounded recomposition implementation preserved both historical local records: the Stage B v4 atom suite passed 32/32 expectations and the unchanged Stage B v3 macro suite passed 110/110 expectations; four future Qwen3-8B + G9 recomposition smoke packets also compiled with zero failures.
notes: The four prepared packets cover two recomposition fixtures with two repetitions each. Packet compilation is infrastructure evidence, not model execution.

claim: Stage B v4-recomposition-smoke is admissible because the local evaluator distinguishes evidence, vocabulary, and gate failures independently without changing prior atom or macro scoring; model transfer under bounded composition remains untested until that smoke runs.
notes: Recommended smoke: Qwen/Qwen3-8B, G9, two fixtures, two repetitions each, four provider calls, with the existing no-retry rule for complete semantic failures.
